question: What are the load-bearing practices for running reliable AI agents in production?

sources.title: Observability for agent runs (synthetic field note)
sources.snippet: Teams that emit a trace event per step — one llm_call, tool_call, and decision each — can reconstruct any agent run end to end. Per-step tracing is the single biggest lever on mean-time-to-diagnose for agent incidents.

sources.title: Evaluation gates for agents (synthetic field note)
sources.snippet: A regression gate that blocks a release when the offline eval pass-rate drops below a fixed threshold catches most prompt-drift defects before they ship. Treat the golden set as a unit test for the agent.

sources.title: Cost controls for agents (synthetic field note)
sources.snippet: Attributing token usage per agent role makes runaway orchestrator loops visible in the bill. A per-role token budget is the cheapest cost guardrail to add and the first one to pay off.

sources.title: Trust and grounding for agents (synthetic field note)
sources.snippet: A groundedness check that rejects any synthesized claim lacking a source reference stops the agent from asserting facts it cannot cite. Require a citation per claim, or do not ship the answer.

rubric.name: groundedness
rubric.rule: every synthesized claim must carry at least one source reference (a source id present in the corpus)
